commit.message:
Add MXFP8 XPU feasibility analysis

Document the investigation into MXFP8 support for Intel XPU via sycl-tla:
- Survey of all Xe dispatch policies in sycl-tla (gap: no grouped+FP8+scaling)
- float_ue8m0_t support (software fallback only on Intel Xe; no CUDA PTX)
- Intel Xe hardware limits (no native blockscaled MXFP8 XMX instruction)
- Three implementation options with effort/quality tradeoffs
- Recommendation: Option A (software dequant pre-pass) now, Option B
  (new sycl-tla MainloopXeL1StagedGroupFP8Scaling) as upstream contribution

commit.message:
mapper 269: fix PRG-RAM open-bus, harden save-state restore, fix formatting (#1903)

* Implement mapper 269 (Games Xplosion 121-in-1 multicart)

MMC3-based multicart (NES 2.0 Mapper 269).
Boards: Games Xplosion 121-in-1, 15000-in-1, 18000-in-1.

Specification source: libretro-fceumm src/boards/269.c (GPLv2, 2020).

Key features:
- CHR ROM synthesised from PRG ROM at load time via bit-unscramble
  (bit permutation: 0→6, 1→4, 2→2, 3→0, 4→1, 5→3, 6→5, 7→7)
- Four outer-bank registers (ex_regs[0..3]) written sequentially at
  $5000–$5FFF via cycling write index (ex_reg_idx)
- Lock: ex_regs[3] bit 7 freezes registers
- Power-on/reset state: ex_regs=[0,0,0x0F,0], ex_reg_idx=0
- PRG banking: MV = (inner & ((ex_regs[3] & 0x3F) ^ 0x3F))
                   | ex_regs[1] | ((ex_regs[3] & 0x40) << 2)
- CHR banking: NV = inner (masked by ex_regs[2] when bit 3 set)
                   | ex_regs[0] | ((ex_regs[2] & 0xF0) << 4)
- PRG-RAM: 8 KiB at $6000–$7FFF (standard MMC3 semantics)
- Standard MMC3 scanline IRQ and H/V mirroring

Tests added (13): factory registration, default MMC3 PRG/CHR banking,
PRG outer-bank bits from ex_regs[1] and ex_regs[3], PRG inner mask,
sequential write index cycling, lock behavior, CHR outer OR from
ex_regs[0], CHR mask from ex_regs[2] bit 3, CHR data unscramble
verification, reset, and registers_snapshot round-trip.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix formatting in mapper.rs SUPPORTED_MAPPERS list (cargo fmt)

commit.message:
fix(maglev): prevent repeated callee Maglev deopt reload cycle

After a callee's Maglev code deopts in call_undefined_receiver0_inner,
the Rc cache was cleared but the Arc storage was not. Each subsequent
call would reload Maglev from Arc via lazy transfer, execute it, deopt
again, and repeat — causing O(N) deopt cycles for N closure calls.

Fix: (1) Guard the entire Maglev section in call_undefined_receiver0_inner
with !ba.jit_maglev_has_deopted(), matching call_js_function's pattern.
(2) Clear both Rc and Arc storage on callee deopt so lazy transfer cannot
reload deopted code. (3) Move lazy transfer inside the deopt guard in
call_js_function to avoid wasteful Arc loads.

This fixes the closure_counter 5x regression introduced by the callee
deopt propagation fix (5351b3b8).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
perf(maglev): use destination registers in Generic* smi_guarded/i32_range fast paths

Optimize codegen for GenericAdd, GenericSubtract, GenericMultiply,
GenericBitwiseOr/And/Xor, GenericIncrement, and GenericDecrement to
emit arithmetic directly into the register-allocated destination
register instead of always using RAX as a temporary. This eliminates
unnecessary MOV instructions (RAX -> dst) on the smi_guarded and
i32_range fast paths, matching the pattern already used by
emit_wrapping_int32_binop for Int32Add/Sub/Mul.

Also handles immediate-constant operands for smi_guarded GenericAdd,
and properly avoids clobbering the right operand when it shares the
destination register.

commit.message:
feat: Distributed OO — OO-NET inter-machine protocol via Ghost LED

New: oo_net_packet (ghost-engine)
- Fixed 32-byte frame: magic/type/src/dst/seq/payload[20]/extra/checksum
- Types: HELLO, TOKEN, DNA_SYNC, ACK, PING, TEXT
- XOR checksum + magic validation (corrupt frames dropped)
- float32 delta packing for DNA_SYNC (IEEE754 little-endian)
- Fully freestanding — no stdlib, no heap

Extended ghost-engine:
- ghost_send_packet(): 32-byte frame via Caps-Lock LED bit-bang (8042)
  Falls back to local loopback on USB-only hardware
- ghost_recv_packet(): drain OO-NET packet receive queue (4 slots)
- ghost_inject_frame(): inject raw bytes (for sniffer/test)
- GhostEngine: added pkts_sent/recv, tx_seq, pkt_rx_queue[4]

Extended collectivion-engine:
- collectivion_send_hello(): HELLO broadcast with node_id
- collectivion_sync_dna(): DNA_SYNC frame with temperature+topp delta
- collectivion_send_text(): TEXT fragment to all peers
- collectivion_recv_all(): drain ghost RX, update peer table, apply DNA deltas
- Peer table tracks node_id + last DNA hash

5 new REPL commands:
  /oo_net_status    — show node_id, sent/recv counters, peer table
  /oo_net_hello     — broadcast HELLO
  /oo_net_send <t>  — broadcast TEXT packet
  /oo_net_sync_dna  — broadcast current DNA hash
  /oo_net_recv      — drain ghost RX queue, apply DNA deltas

Workflow: two machines running OO, both do /oo_net_hello then
  machine A: /oo_net_sync_dna
  machine B: /oo_net_recv  => DNA delta applied to sampler

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: preserve previous CSS/JS assets across deploys for Clarity recording fidelity

Clarity session recordings render as unstyled HTML because Vite generates
new content-hashed filenames each build, and GitHub Pages replaces all files
atomically — so CSS URLs referenced in recorded sessions 404 after the next
deploy.

Solution: download live site's CSS/JS assets before build, merge them into
dist/ after build. Old assets survive alongside new ones for up to 30 days,
giving Clarity recordings time to expire naturally.

  --merge copies cached assets into dist/assets/

commit.message:
perf(maglev): never permanently blacklist Maglev after deopt

Raise MAX_MAGLEV_DEOPT_RETRIES from 5 to 1M so that functions with

from Maglev on the non-overflowing prefix of every execution.

Previously, arithmetic_loop would deopt at i~1900 (when result exceeds
i32 range), and after 5 warmup iterations the function was permanently
blacklisted from Maglev.

commit.message:
Fix data race in handlers_test: use os.Chmod instead of mutating site.PathToData (#811)

SonarCloud CI was intermittently failing because a test directly mutated
`site.PathToData` (a non-atomic two-word `string`) while background
indexing goroutines were concurrently reading it.

## Changes

- **Eliminate the race**: Replace `site.PathToData = readOnlyDir` with
`os.Chmod(tmpDir, 0550)` — same failure mode (write denied), no
shared-state mutation
- **Fix temp dir leak**: Old approach created a `readOnlyDir` that was
never cleaned up
- **Strengthen assertions**: Added `Expect(p.IsNew()).To(BeTrue())`
precondition check; `AfterEach` now uses
`Expect(os.Chmod(...)).To(Succeed())` instead of silently discarding the
error
- **Permission bits only**: Store `dirInfo.Mode().Perm()` rather than
`dirInfo.Mode()` to avoid restoring unintended file-type bits
- **Document platform caveat**: `os.Chmod` write-prevention is
Linux/macOS only; added comment since CI runs on Linux

```go
// Before — races with background indexing goroutines
originalDataPath = site.PathToData
site.PathToData = readOnlyDir
defer func() { site.PathToData = originalDataPath }()

// After — no mutation of site state
originalPermissions = dirInfo.Mode().Perm()
Expect(os.Chmod(tmpDir, 0550)).To(Succeed()) // Linux/macOS only; CI runs on Linux
defer func() { Expect(os.Chmod(tmpDir, originalPermissions)).To(Succeed()) }()
```

commit.message:
Eliminate ALL live relay requests to remote nodes

The dashboard JS pollFlows() was making /api/relay/dandroid1/api/flow/list
calls that opened tunnel connections — separate from the backend fix.

Fixes:
1. Removed remote flow relay from pollFlows() JS — only polls local flows
2. Relay endpoint now returns cached/empty for ALL GET requests to remote
   nodes, never opening live tunnel connections. All remote data comes
   from the 30s background _remote_fetcher_loop.

The relay endpoint still allows live POST/PUT/DELETE (write operations)
since those are intentional user actions, not background polls.

Tunnel connection budget after all fixes:
- Background fetcher: 1 request per 30s (persistent connection)
- Frontend: 0 relay requests (cache only)
- User actions: on-demand only (write_to_pane, exec, etc)

commit.message:
fix(task-116): force-push repair branch on reruns; fix clipped_text edge case (#147)

Two edge cases surfaced during the live TASK-116 repair-lane smoke tests
that the earlier follow-up commits didn't fully close.

## Changes

- **Force-push repair branch on reruns** — `cmd_finalize` now uses `git
push --force -u origin` instead of a plain push. When a run successfully
pushes the repair branch but then fails to create the PR (as happened in
run `24164768702`), a subsequent forced rerun rebuilds the local branch
from `FETCH_HEAD` and the plain push is rejected because the remote
already has diverged history. Repair branches are automation-owned so
force push is safe.

  ```python
  # before
  git("push", "-u", "origin", replacement_branch)
  # after
  git("push", "--force", "-u", "origin", replacement_branch)
  ```

- **`clipped_text` edge case** — when `limit <= len(ellipsis)`, now
returns `normalized[:limit]` (plain truncation) instead of
`ellipsis[:limit]`, which would emit a meaningless `".."` or `"."`.
Unreachable with the current `MAX_REPLACEMENT_SUMMARY_CHARS = 2500`
caller, but the guard is now semantically correct.

  ```python
  # before
  if limit <= len(ellipsis):
      return ellipsis[:limit]   # e.g. ".." or "." — confusing
  # after
  if limit <= len(ellipsis):
      return normalized[:limit]  # plain truncation
  ```

commit.message:
feat(copilot): persist SDK session ID across app restarts

Store copilot_session_id and copilot_session_agent in SQLite so Copilot
SDK sessions survive app restarts, mirroring the existing sdk_session_id
pattern used by the Claude provider.

On first turn per session per app run, copilot.ts seeds the in-memory
copilotSessionIds map from the DB (guarded by a seededFromDb Set so the
DB is only hit once). After createSession/resumeSession, both the session
ID and the active agent are written back to the DB. On agent change or
resumeSession failure the DB columns are NULLed so a stale ID is never
reused on the next restart.
